title: Expand test suite: supervisor, daemon integration, and remaining coverage gaps
description:
## Context

Initial test suite was added (83 tests, commit 0b87614) covering health, runtime, DNS, scheduler, daemon routes, and plist. Several packages still lack tests.

## Remaining Work

### Supervisor tests (0% coverage, 336 lines)
- Refactor to inject a process factory (instead of direct os/exec) for testability
- Test restart policies: always, on-failure, never
- Test max restarts limit
- Test exponential backoff (5s → 10s → 20s → 60s cap)
- Test Stop (SIGTERM to process group) and Kill (SIGKILL)
- Test StopAll and IsRunning/PID helpers

### Daemon integration tests (Run/startAll/shutdown at 0%)
- Test state file locking (flock-based exclusivity)
- Test clean slate recovery (orphan container cleanup)
- Test signal handling (SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful shutdown)
- Test statsPoller and logPoller goroutines
- Test proxy setup

### Coverage gaps in tested packages
- daemon routes: handleRun (0%), handleStatsByName (33%), handleApply execute path
- scheduler: Start(), runSchedule(), execute() — goroutine-based, need time mocking
- config: validateHealthCheck (29%), more edge cases in validate()
- plist: Install/Uninstall need HOME override or path injection refactor

## Approach

1. Extract `ProcessStarter` interface from supervisor for mock injection
2. Use `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())` for plist file I/O tests
3. Consider `clockwork` or similar for time-dependent scheduler tests
4. Add `-race` flag to CI test command

## Current Coverage Baseline

| Package    | Coverage |
|------------|----------|
| health     | 95.6%    |
| runtime    | 91.7%    |
| dns        | 91.2%    |
| logbuf     | 81.8%    |
| config     | 77.4%    |
| status     | 73.2%    |
| scheduler  | 44.4%    |
| daemon     | 37.6%    |
| plist      | 3.7%     |
| supervisor | 0%       |

title: CLI and API support for replica containers
description: Ensure replicated containers appear in skiff ps with their expanded names. Add skiff ps --group to show replicas grouped by template. Add GET /v1/replicas endpoint that returns replica groups with counts and status summary. Ensure skiff up/down/restart work with individual replica names (coder-1) and template names (coder = all replicas). Ensure skiff scale <name> <n> adjusts replica count at runtime via POST /v1/scale.

title: Phase 2a: Agent identity + heartbeat system
description: Agent registration API: POST /v1/agents (register), GET /v1/agents (list), POST /v1/agents/{id}/heartbeat. Agents register on container boot. Heartbeat-based liveness: if no heartbeat within 2x interval, mark agent as unresponsive. Unresponsive agents get restarted. AgentStatus tracks: name, swarm, state (idle/working/completed/failed), current task, branch, tasks completed count.
close_reason: Moved to separate tools (bosun / Agent Mail)

title: Phase 1b: WorktreeManager - git worktree lifecycle
description: New internal/swarm/worktree.go. WorktreeManager manages git worktrees tied to agent containers. EnsureRepo() clones or validates existing repo. Create() makes a new worktree on branch {prefix}{agent-name}. Destroy() removes worktree and branch. List() returns active worktrees. Uses os/exec to call git directly. Worktrees created under configurable base dir (default ~/swarm/worktrees/{agent-name}).

title: Fix container restart leaving orphaned container IDs
description:
## Problem

When restarting a container (`skiff restart <name>`), `Stop()` in `AppleRuntime` calls `container stop` then falls back to `container delete` only on failure. But `container stop` succeeds (exit 0) without removing the container ID, so the subsequent `Run()` fails with "container with id <name> already exists". Requires manual `container rm` to fix.

## Root Cause

`AppleRuntime.Stop()` (`internal/runtime/apple.go:95-107`) treats `delete` as a fallback for when `stop` fails. Since `stop` succeeds, `delete` is never called, leaving the container ID registered.

## Fix

`Stop()` should always stop then delete, ignoring "not found" errors from delete.

## Also affected

- `handleRestart` in `internal/daemon/routes.go:368-384`
- Clean slate recovery in `internal/daemon/daemon.go`
- Auto-restart from health checks (`daemon.go:101`)

title: Add replicas + template expansion to container config
description: Add optional 'replicas' field (int, default 0/absent = singleton) to ContainerConfig. When replicas > 0, expand into N container configs at parse time: {name}-1 through {name}-N. Support {name} template variable in volume paths and env values, expanded per replica. Validate: replicas >= 0, {name} only in volumes/env. No new top-level config section -- this extends existing containers.

title: Phase 2c: File lease system for conflict prevention
description: Advisory file leases: POST /v1/agents/{id}/lease to acquire, DELETE to release. Leases are path or glob patterns with TTL. Exclusive leases prevent other agents from claiming overlapping paths. Lease state stored in SharedState. Leases auto-released when agent completes task or times out. Pre-claim check: before assigning task, verify no file overlap with active leases.
close_reason: Moved to separate tools (bosun / Agent Mail)

title: Phase 1c: SwarmManager - container pool lifecycle
description: New internal/swarm/manager.go. SwarmManager creates/destroys pools of containers from a SwarmConfig template. Start() creates N worktrees + N containers with volume mounts. Stop() tears down containers + worktrees. Scale() adjusts replica count (add or remove). Agents named {swarm}-{index}. Each container gets skiff.swarm={name} label. Containers registered in SharedState as regular containers. Integrates with existing health check system.
close_reason: Replaced by simple replicas expansion in config

title: Ad-hoc container spawning API for agent sandboxes
description: Add POST /v1/containers/run that accepts an inline container spec (image, command, volumes, env, cpus, memory, labels, timeout_secs, remove). Does NOT require container to exist in skiff.yml. Auto-generates name if not provided. Injects skiff.parent label from auth context or request. Auto-kills after timeout_secs. Auto-removes on exit if remove=true. Add parent-child tracking: when parent container stops, kill all children. Add GET /v1/containers?parent={name} to list spawned containers. This is the critical path for OpenClaw agents spawning sandbox containers -- Apple containers use Mach IPC so there is no socket to mount into Linux guests.

title: Phase 2b: Task dispatch with Beads integration
description: TaskDispatcher reads from Beads DB to find ready tasks (unblocked, priority-ordered). Agents claim tasks via POST /v1/agents/{id}/claim. On claim: mark in_progress in Beads, assign to agent. On complete: close in Beads, trigger on_complete action (PR or merge). Task board API: GET /v1/swarms/{name}/tasks. Ingest API: POST /v1/swarms/{name}/tasks to import from Beads or GitHub Issues.

title: Phase 3: Result collection + auto-PR/merge
description: On agent task completion, WorktreeManager handles result: on_complete=pr creates GitHub PR via gh CLI (title from task, body from agent output). on_complete=merge fast-forwards target branch. on_complete=none leaves branch for manual handling. Track results: which agent, task ID, duration, branch, PR URL. Reset worktree to main after completion so agent can take next task.
